title: [Lower SCF/Standard to Calyx] Support for memref.global
body:
Hi! I'd like to convert some SCF/Affine MLIR to Verilog, and going through an intermediate Calyx to HW to Verilog path.

I'm running into some failure to support `memref.global` and was curious for some advice on a better way around this.

A simple reproducer looks like:
```
module {
  memref.global "private" constant @output : memref<2xi32> = dense<[0, 1]>
  func.func @main(%arg0: index) -> i32 {
    %9 = memref.get_global @output : memref<2xi32>
    %arg2 = affine.load %9[%arg0] : memref<2xi32>

    return %arg2 : i32
  }
}
```

Command & Logs
```
$ circt-opt reproducer.memref_global.mlir --lower-scf-to-calyx
reproducer.memref_global.mlir:4:10: error: Unhandled operation during BuildOpGroups()
    %9 = memref.get_global @output : memref<2xi32>
         ^
reproducer.memref_global.mlir:4:10: note: see current operation: %0 = "memref.get_global"() {name = @output} : () -> memref<2xi32>
```

This points to just lack of support, although I do know there is some support for some memref ops; e.g. https://github.com/llvm/circt/pull/1897

I think in theory I could flatten and convert these to `arith.constant`. Or maybe there's a better conversion or pass to do before this? 

Thanks!

cc @j2kun
